description: A broad category of dishes in which chicken is cooked in a spiced sauce or gravy. Recipes vary greatly by region and may feature tomato, onion, yogurt, coconut milk, or a dry spice blend as the main flavor base.
preparation_steps: Brown or lightly sear the chicken if the recipe calls for it.
Cook the onion, garlic, ginger, and spices to build the base.
Add the sauce ingredients such as tomato, yogurt, or coconut milk.
Simmer the chicken until fully cooked and the sauce is flavorful.

nutrition.carbohydrates: Low to moderate; higher when served with rice or bread
nutrition.fat: Moderate; higher in creamy or coconut-based versions

health_advice.benefits: Can be protein-rich and flavorful, and often includes aromatic spices and onions.
health_advice.risks: Salt, oil, and saturated fat can be high in restaurant or creamy versions.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use lean chicken, moderate oil, and pair it with vegetables instead of relying only on large rice portions.

dietary_notes.note: Chicken curry is not vegetarian or vegan. Many versions are naturally gluten-free, but packaged spice mixes or accompaniments should be checked.

allergens: Milk if yogurt or cream is used
Possible tree nuts in some regional curry pastes or gravies
Possible gluten in packaged mixes or naan served alongside
